mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is moving down from a restrictive level.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative during months of falling FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above the prior 3-month pace forces real-rate expectations higher and raises the probability of policy tightening, which lowers near-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-surprise episodes deliver positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal loses sign consistency for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE reflects labor-market deterioration, weakens household income expectations, and increases recession pricing, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if increases in UNRATE fail to precede negative 20-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal's rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a decline in UNRATE indicates broad payroll growth and improving labor absorption, which supports consumption and raises S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-growth and falling-unemployment condition does not outperform either single proxy alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in most triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO signals rising real output and factory utilization, which supports earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO changes fail to align with higher 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect reverses in more than one major rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending, which improves revenue growth expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS expansion triggers do not produce above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal remains negative across two successive quarterly evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rebounds while RSAFS is also rising, housing activity and retail spending jointly indicate broad final-demand strength that should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-RSAFS improvement does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered 60-day returns are not positive more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a low or falling FEDFUNDS rate that coincides with CPIAUCSL easing implies a pause in inflation pressure and policy stress, which supports a positive S&P 500 response over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below its 3-month moving average and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation falls below 3.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint easing-disinflation condition does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears once either proxy is removed.
